message:
refactor(storage): replace LIKE search with case-insensitive instr()

The text-search branch of the issue list query previously used:

  AND (title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\' OR description LIKE ? ESCAPE '\'
       OR id LIKE ? ESCAPE '\')

with each parameter set to escape_like_pattern(trimmed) wrapped in
'%...%'. That form pulled in the LIKE escape machinery purely to
defang the user-supplied needle's own '%' and '_' characters, even
though we only ever did substring match — never glob match — so the
escape ceremony was protecting against capabilities we never used.

Switch to instr(lower(col), ?) > 0 over the same three columns, with
the needle lowercased once in Rust. Benefits:

  - No LIKE escape rules to get wrong: the needle is treated as a
    literal substring by definition, so '%' and '_' lose all
    meaning automatically.
  - Case-insensitive without UPPER/LOWER on both sides, and without
    requiring COLLATE NOCASE on the columns.
  - One allocation (the lowercased needle) instead of three String
    formats per query.
  - Drops escape_like_pattern() from the call site.

Behaviour change for callers: search is now ASCII-case-insensitive
where it was previously case-sensitive (LIKE without NOCASE). This
matches user expectation for "find issues containing X" and aligns
with how list_issues_by_text already behaves elsewhere.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
